canonical_question: Why are internal build cost estimates systematically 2-5x too low?
aliases: we can build it cheaper fallacy
constraints: The 2-5x multiplier is a cross-industry average — regulated industries skew higher
Reference class forecasting requires historical data many orgs lack
Some costs (opportunity cost, org drag) are genuinely hard to quantify
Planning fallacy is cognitive bias requiring structural process changes
Expert estimates still subject to same directional biases

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs overall build vs buy framework, not estimation bias
User needs general build/buy/partner decision tree
User needs to estimate specific costs, not diagnose bias

related_to.label: Build vs Buy for Enterprise Software
Build vs Buy vs Partner Decision Tree
Build vs Buy for Integration Layer

often_confused_with.label: Build vs Buy for Enterprise Software (decision framework, not estimation bias)

inputs_needed.question: What is the user's situation with build cost estimation?
options: Evaluating an internal team's claim that building is cheaper than buying
Trying to understand why a past build project exceeded its budget by 2-5x
Seeking debiasing techniques for more accurate software cost estimation
Building a business case and needs realistic cost multipliers for internal builds
